mapping.a_element: optimal information transmission capacity of a transcriptional regulatory module
mapping.b_element: maximal information capacity of an optical fiber channel

mapping.a_element: matching relations between dynamic range of transcription factor concentrations, input/output relation, and noise levels
mapping.b_element: relation between signal power, nonlinear propagation effects, and multiplicative noise in optical fiber
theoretical limit to information capacity due to nonlinearities

mapping.a_element: noise in gene expression (binding and transcription)
mapping.b_element: multiplicative noise in the nonlinear optical channel

mapping.a_element: input/output relation of the regulatory module
mapping.b_element: nonlinear channel model (mapped to a linear channel with multiplicative noise)

brief_justification: Both papers analyze the information capacity of a communication channel under constraints: transcriptional regulation in biology and optical fiber in engineering. They share the underlying machinery of determining the theoretical maximum information transmission by matching system parameters (dynamic range/noise in A, signal power/nonlinearities in B) and modeling the channel as a noisy input/output process, with nonlinearities or noise limiting capacity.
